reference_panel: Parker et al. 2017 Cell Reports (161 breeds, 1355 dogs, lifted to canFam4)
note: Supervised SCOPE (ALStructure) on 102,365 LD-pruned SNPs genotyped directly from BAM. The dog is known to be a Jamthund (Swedish Elkhound). The Parker 2017 reference panel contains Norwegian Elkhound (NELK) but not Jamthund as a separate breed — these two breeds are closely related spitz-type dogs, explaining the 95.3% NELK ancestry. Minor components (Maltese 1.8%, Brussels Griffon 1.7%) likely reflect noise or shared ancient ancestry rather than true admixture.
